# Test mcp2go with MCP tool descriptions
#
# This test generates Go code from MCP tool descriptions

# Create a weather tool description
-- weather_tool.json --
{
  "name": "get_weather",
  "description": "Get the current weather for a location",
  "inputSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "location": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "The city name, e.g. 'New York, NY'"
      },
      "units": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "Temperature units",
        "enum": ["celsius", "fahrenheit"],
        "default": "celsius"
      }
    },
    "required": ["location"]
  },
  "returnType": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "temperature": {
        "type": "number",
        "description": "Current temperature"
      },
      "humidity": {
        "type": "number",
        "description": "Humidity percentage"
      },
      "conditions": {
        "type": "string",
        "description": "Weather conditions"
      }
    },
    "required": ["temperature", "conditions"]
  }
}

# Generate code from MCP tool
exec go run .. -type mcp weather_tool.json
stdout 'Auto-detected input type: mcp'
stdout 'Generated: get_weather_tool.go'

# Check the generated file
exec cat get_weather_tool.go
stdout 'package generated'
stdout 'type GetWeatherTool interface'
stdout 'Execute\(ctx context.Context, input \*GetWeatherInput\) \(\*GetWeatherOutput, error\)'

# Check input type
stdout 'type GetWeatherInput struct'
stdout 'Location string `json:"location"`'
stdout 'Units \*string `json:"units,omitempty"`'

# Check output type  
stdout 'type GetWeatherOutput struct'
stdout 'Temperature float64 `json:"temperature"`'
stdout 'Conditions string `json:"conditions"`'
stdout 'Humidity \*float64 `json:"humidity,omitempty"`'

# Check implementation stub
stdout 'type GetWeatherImpl struct{}'
stdout 'func \(t \*GetWeatherImpl\) Execute\(ctx context.Context'

# Generate with custom package name
exec go run .. -package weather weather_tool.json
stdout 'Generated: get_weather_tool.go'
exec cat get_weather_tool.go
stdout 'package weather'

# Generate with custom output directory
mkdir generated
exec go run .. -output generated weather_tool.json  
stdout 'Generated: generated/get_weather_tool.go'
exec cat generated/get_weather_tool.go
stdout 'package generated'

# Generate with custom filename
exec go run .. -name custom_weather weather_tool.json
stdout 'Generated: custom_weather.go'
exec cat custom_weather.go
stdout 'type GetWeatherTool interface'
